selection_criterion: operator-locked: EXACTLY 3 substrates; >=1 contagion-class (PC2/PC3); >=1 funding/liquidity-class (PC1); >=1 market/information-class (PC4/PC5); the 3 collectively cover >=4 of 6 P2 crisis windows; NO substrate may require real interbank transaction microdata (D-002J public-source only; cross-asset coherence != interbank funding network per Brunetti e-MID).

substrates.update_rule: Maturing short-term funding is rolled over each period at the prevailing market funding rate; rollover ratio degrades as the funding rate rises above baseline, and the funding gap accumulates whenever rollover falls below the solvency-feasible floor.
substrates.shock_channel: exogenous funding-rate stress jump (public SOFR/Treasury-spread spike) that decays exponentially, raising the cost of rolling over short-term liabilities.
substrates.expected_observable_signature: On a true funding-stress window the funding_stress_index steps up at onset and rollover_failure_count accelerates monotonically while broader equity/credit panic stays muted (CW3 'narrow funding signature without market panic' constraint).

interpretable_parameters.base_rollover_ratio: calm-state fraction of maturing funding successfully rolled over
interpretable_parameters.rollover_solvency_floor: rollover ratio below which the institution accrues an unmet funding gap
interpretable_parameters.funding_rate_stress_jump: size of the exogenous funding-rate spike at onset
interpretable_parameters.stress_decay: exponential decay rate of the funding-rate spike (policy-backstop relief speed)

substrates.non_identifiability_boundary: Cannot distinguish a genuine rollover-stress episode from an exogenous policy-rate hike of equal magnitude using the rate path alone; only the joint funding_gap accumulation under a constant solvency floor separates them, and that separation is unidentifiable from public aggregates if the floor itself moved.
substrates.power_feasibility_path: Effect is a step + accumulating-count signature; qualitatively detectable at moderate horizon (>= ~60 periods) and stress-jump >= ~1x baseline-spread; P7 computes n_min and minimum detectable stress_jump.
forbidden_claims: this substrate validates real-bank systemic risk
public funding-rate spread proves a physical interbank funding network
passing this substrate authorises a canonical run

substrates.update_rule: A DebtRank-style impact cascade propagates a seed balance-sheet shock across a max-entropy-reconstructed exposure network in damped rounds, with each node's equity loss driven by the impact-weighted losses of its counterparties until the cascade saturates.
substrates.shock_channel: seed impairment of the most-connected nodes (fraction seed_shock_fraction at magnitude seed_shock_magnitude) propagated through reconstructed exposure weights.
substrates.expected_observable_signature: On a true contagion window the cascade_impaired_fraction rises in a damped-sigmoid shape from the seed set and the systemic_loss_index saturates well above the no-default null floor.

interpretable_parameters.exposure_intensity: aggregate public exposure intensity scaling the reconstructed impact weights
interpretable_parameters.recovery_rate: fraction of a defaulted counterparty's exposure recovered (loss-given-default = 1 - recovery_rate)
interpretable_parameters.seed_shock_magnitude: initial equity-loss fraction on seed nodes
interpretable_parameters.damping: round-to-round attenuation of further loss propagation

substrates.non_identifiability_boundary: Cannot distinguish true bilateral contagion from common-exposure co-movement: the reconstructed network is a max-entropy guess from public aggregates, so a cascade pattern is observationally equivalent to a shared-asset shock and does NOT identify the true interbank topology.
substrates.power_feasibility_path: Cascade saturation gap vs no-default null is large for exposure_intensity above the percolation threshold; qualitatively detectable with n_nodes >= ~20; P7 computes n_min and the minimum detectable exposure_intensity.
forbidden_claims: this substrate validates real-bank systemic risk
a reconstructed-network cascade proves the true interbank exposure topology
passing this substrate authorises a canonical run

substrates.update_rule: A sticky latent two-regime (calm vs stressed) process governs the conditional variance of a market-wide stress index and the mean-reverting target of a composite credit-spread level, so a regime switch jointly lifts realised volatility and widens the spread.
substrates.shock_channel: latent regime switch at onset (persistence-controlled) that simultaneously raises conditional volatility and the credit-spread reversion target.
substrates.expected_observable_signature: On a true market-wide stress window realised_volatility steps from calm to stress level and credit_spread_level mean-reverts upward to the stressed target, jointly and persistently.

interpretable_parameters.calm_vol: calm-regime conditional volatility of the market-wide index
interpretable_parameters.regime_persistence: probability the stressed regime persists period-to-period (stickiness)

substrates.non_identifiability_boundary: Cannot attribute a joint vol/spread regime shift to any specific underlying cause (funding, contagion, or pure risk-aversion); it detects the market-wide regime state, not its mechanism, and cannot localise stress to any institution.
substrates.power_feasibility_path: Two-regime separation is large when stress_vol/calm_vol >= ~3 and stress_spread/calm_spread >= ~3; qualitatively detectable at horizon >= ~80 periods; P7 computes n_min and the minimum detectable vol/spread ratio.
forbidden_claims: this substrate validates real-bank systemic risk
a market-wide vol/spread regime proves institution-level distress
passing this substrate authorises a canonical run

cross_asset_interbank_scope_guard.statement: Per Brunetti et al. (e-MID) the physical interbank funding network CONTRACTS in crisis while cross-asset correlation networks EXPAND; therefore cross-asset coherence does NOT prove interbank funding contagion and NO admitted substrate may require real interbank transaction microdata.

claim_boundary: 3 financial-mechanistic substrate candidates admitted; each is P1B-source-bound (>=2 VERIFIED/PARTIAL sources), P2-window-bound, P3-adapter-bound, P4-control-compatible. Substrates are GENERATIVE models, NOT real-data fits. Cross-asset coherence != interbank funding proof (Brunetti e-MID). No real-bank validation; no canonical run authorised.
